figures.caption: Table 1. Quantitative comparision on the Toys4K-CL benchmark. We report CLIP similarity (↑) and Fréchet Distance scores computed on Inception and PointNet++ features (↓). Forgetting (%) measures base-class degradation after novel training. Results are reported on TRELLIS-XL and Shap-E backbones. The best results are in bold, and the second-best are underlined.
Table 4. Class-wise Evaluation on Novel-Class Assets (CLIP (↑)) for both TRELLIS-XL and Shap-E. Best scores are highlighted in bold, whereas our ReConText3D results are highlighted in purple.

Figure 3. Qualitative comparision of continual text-to-3D baselines on base class assets: From left to right: Ground Truth, Base Training, Fine-tuning, L2-SP, and our method. Results on TRELLIS are shown on the top, while Shap-E is shown on the bottom.

Table 3. Class-wise Evaluation (CLIP (↑)) on Base-Class assets for TRELLIS-XL and Shap-E. Best scores are highlighted in bold, whereas our ReConText3D results are highlighted in purple. Best scores are shown excluding Base Training.

Figure 2. Overview of the ReConText3D framework. Base captions are encoded with CLIP and selected via k-center sampling under a count-aware budget to form replay memory R, which is combined with novel data to incrementally train GθB∪N while preserving baseclass synthesis.
Figure 11. Qualitative comparison of continual text-to-3D baselines against ReConText3D (Ours) on base class assets using Shap-E.
Figure 8. Number of base class assets (replay examplers) returned by our count-aware budget allocation
Figure 6. Qualitative comparision of novel class assets across baselines.
Figure 12. Qualitative comparison of continual text-to-3D baselines against ReConText3D (Ours) on novel class assets using Shap-E.
Figure 10. Qualitative comparison of continual text-to-3D baselines against ReConText3D (Ours) on novel class assets using TRELLISXL.
Figure 7. Class distribution statistics for the Toys4K-CL benchmark (train and test). Both splits exhibit similar long-tailed behaviour.
Figure 9. Qualitative comparison of continual text-to-3D baselines against ReConText3D (Ours) on base class assets using TRELLIS-XL.
